post_fix_verification.qa301_backfill_completion: PARTIAL OK -- schema bump / soft_score recalc / overall_passed re-aggregation numerically correct, BUT (1) gate_check.passed / failed_conditions / layer2.passed not updated -> 11 S-10 invariant violations, (2) 846 skipped strategies left at 6.1.0 -> schema cohort split, (3) History DB stale for 1 strategy, (4) Phase 4.5 advisory unrecorded, (5) flip-cause attribution (3 dup-fix vs 12 FLOOR) not documented
post_fix_verification.flip_count_verification: 15 OK (breakdown: 3 dup-fix-driven + 12 FLOOR-driven; of these 11 had old overall_passed=True -> 11 S-10 invariant violations after backfill)
post_fix_verification.math_recompute_spot_check: ATLAS-2026-0508-037 measured: soft_score = 0.5747709999 / 0.90 = 0.6386344444 -> matches recorded value exactly. FLOOR=0.90 correctly applied by _compute_soft_score_redistributed
post_fix_verification.fts_live_impact: 0 confirmed (all 15 flipped absent from trading_platform/strategies/imported; result_parser.py:211-216 safe-side composes bool(overall_passed) AND bool(passed) so downstream BacktestGateResult.overall_passed correctly False)
post_fix_verification.skip_count_verification: 846 skip_no_tier2 are all pre-Phase-2 format with no gate_check.tier2 and no tier1; 0 cases of overall_passed=True among them, skip is legitimate. Absence of schema bump captured as QA-502

findings.title: QA-301 backfill violates S-10 invariant: 11 strategies left with gate_check.passed=True but gate_check.overall_passed=False
findings.description: scripts/backfill_v6_2_0_soft_score.py:188-195 updates only gate_check.overall_passed and does NOT update the legacy alias key gate_check.passed (which must always equal decision.overall_passed by the S-10 invariant). Of 15 flipped strategies, 11 now have gate_check.passed=True while gate_check.overall_passed=False -- a state impossible from runner.py. Additionally, the cluster_cap_exceeded label (runner.py:1513) and the Soft Score (less than 0.70) label (runner.py:1062) are not appended to failed_conditions which remains empty. tests/golden/test_gate.py:648-664 and tests/unit/test_runner_duplicate_gate.py:98-118 explicitly test this invariant. Downstream consumers reading gate_check.passed (list_clones.py / detect_clones.py / rejudge_gate scripts / human review / atlas-status) will report PASS incorrectly. 6.1.0 cohort had 0 S-10 violations; 6.2.0 cohort has 11/607 -- proves regression is backfill-induced.
findings.reproduction: For each of 15 flipped GIDs, assert gate_check.passed == gate_check.overall_passed: 11 fail. Aggregated: 6.2.0 cohort violations = 11, 6.1.0 cohort = 0.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to backfill_v6_2_0_soft_score.py:188-195: (a) gate_check[passed] = new_overall_passed (S-10 sync); (b) if tier2 flipped True->False, append Soft Score (less than 0.70) to failed_conditions; (c) if is_duplicate=True and overall_passed flipped to False, append cluster_cap_exceeded if not present; (d) if layer2 is not None and error is None, set layer2[passed]=new_overall_passed. Re-run on 11 affected strategies. Add parametrized test in tests/unit/test_runner_duplicate_gate.py covering all 11.
findings.impact_scope: Live impact 0 (all 15 flipped not imported to FTS; result_parser.py:211-216 safe-side composes so BacktestGateResult.overall_passed remains correct False). Risk is downstream tools reading gate_check.passed directly.

findings.title: QA-301 backfill leaves 846 skipped strategies at 6.1.0; schema cohort split into 607/846
findings.description: backfill_v6_2_0_soft_score.py:135-145 skips strategies without gate_check.tier2 or .components and does NOT update metrics_schema_version. Result: 846/1453 (L1 FAIL / pre-Phase-2 format) remain at 6.1.0 while 607 are now at 6.2.0. CLAUDE.md Q4 (METRICS_SCHEMA_VERSION cross-generation warning) requires mismatched schemas produce a cannot directly compare warning. atlas-loop / comparator.py / convergence will now warn on 846 strategies. Even if Tier 2 logic is unchanged for skipped strategies (so schema bump may not be strictly required), the reach of QA-301 spec change becomes ambiguous.
findings.suggested_fix: Option A (recommended): also update result[metrics_schema_version]=TARGET_SCHEMA in the skip path; separate the Tier 2 computation flag from the schema marker (rename status to skip_no_tier2_schema_bumped). Option B: keep current behavior; document in docs/spec_change_log.md that this change does not apply to the 846 strategies without Tier 2, so schema bump is also not applied; add a flag to comparator.py warning logic to treat 6.1.0 vs 6.2.0 as equivalent for non-Tier-2 indicators.
findings.impact_scope: atlas-loop parent-vs-child improvement calc may misfire on 846 strategies, comparator.py / list_clones.py cross-generation display inconsistent. Live impact 0.

findings.title: QA-301 backfill creates fresh layer2.passed=True / overall_passed=False inconsistency (H-NEW-1 anti-pattern recurrence)
findings.description: result_parser.py:206-216 comment explicitly states the overall_passed=False vs layer2.passed=True state exists only in pre-S-10 result.json files and is preserved only for backwards compatibility. QA-301 backfill does not touch layer2 at all, so it creates the same inconsistency in 11 strategies. runner.py:1500-1502 explicitly sets layer2.passed=False on dup-cap revert, with tests/unit/test_runner_duplicate_gate.py:92-96 covering it. weakness_detector / fts_selection_methodology / atlas-status will misread Layer 2 PASS from these strategies.
findings.reproduction: All 11 flipped strategies measured to have layer2.passed=True while overall_passed=False.
findings.suggested_fix: Combine with QA-501 fix: when overall_passed flips to False in backfill, also set result[layer2][passed]=False if layer2 is present and error is None. Update result_parser.py H-NEW-1 comment to note 2026-05-30 QA-503 closed the backfill-side inconsistency.
findings.impact_scope: Live impact 0. Skill-layer weakness_detector / fts_selection_methodology / atlas-status may misread Layer 2 status, distorting improvement recommendations.

findings.title: tier2.components.contribution stays as pre-redistribution value: sum(contributions) != soft_score; gap shrunk to ~1.11x by QA-301 but not zero
findings.description: _tier2_component (runner.py:700-712) stores contribution = normalized times weight (pre-redistribution), but soft_score is the redistributed value. Example ATLAS-2026-0508-037: sum(contributions)=0.5748 vs soft_score=0.6386. Long-standing design property since v5.5.0 (not a backfill regression), but QA-301 FLOOR=0.90 shrinks the max gap from 1.43x to 1.11x while not eliminating it. Human reviewers / spec.md generators that sum contributions to verify soft_score will see mismatches without documented explanation.
findings.reproduction: Measured: sum(contributions)=0.5748, soft_score=0.6386, valid_weight=0.82, FLOOR=0.90, gap=0.6386/0.5748=1.111.
findings.suggested_fix: Option A: add redistributed_contributions field to tier2 storing (normalized*weight)/effective_weight so sum=soft_score is guaranteed. Option B (recommended, low cost): add comment above runner.py:711 contribution= line saying pre-redistribution raw value, effective soft_score recalculated by _compute_soft_score_redistributed.
findings.impact_scope: Human review and spec.md generation mismatch. Live impact 0.

findings.title: QA-301 backfill does not trigger atlas history sync; atlas_history.db gate_result cache stale (1 measured)
findings.description: backfill_v6_2_0_soft_score.py only updates result.json and never re-syncs data/atlas_history.db via atlas.history.sync. generations table gate_result.passed / overall_passed drift from file. Measured ATLAS-2026-0520-041 has DB.gate_result.overall_passed=True while file is False. context_builder / list_clones.py / atlas-history / atlas-compare reading from DB will get stale data. Full 6.2.0 cohort scan: only 1 strategy has DB=True / file=False but logically all 11 are candidates (the other 10 have stale-but-False DB values that coincidentally agree).
findings.reproduction: sqlite3 SELECT gate_result FROM generations WHERE strategy_id=ATLAS-2026-0520-041 -> overall_passed=True; file -> False.
findings.suggested_fix: (1) Invoke atlas history sync at the end of backfill_v6_2_0_soft_score.py main() (subprocess or direct import). (2) Immediate mitigation: instruct user to run ATLAS/.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m atlas.main history sync --all, document in spec_change_log. (3) Add sync_required flag to backfill script template to prevent recurrence.
findings.impact_scope: atlas-loop generation selection / clone detection / spec.md generation may use stale data. Currently 1 strategy affected but post-QA-501/503 fixes may increase. Live impact 0.

findings.title: Phase 4.5 advisory unrecorded: 3 of 15 flips are is_duplicate-driven NOT QA-301 FLOOR-driven
findings.description: logs/backfill_v6_2_0_soft_score.json shows 3 of 15 flipped (0506-026, 0507-010, 0507-013) have soft_score_delta=0.0. The old overall_passed=True -> new=False flip is driven by duplicate_check.is_duplicate=True (cluster overlap with 0506-024/036), NOT by FLOOR change. These flips are backfill incidentally repairing pre-QA-003 (9th audit) state. docs/spec_change_log.md attributes all 15 flips to QA-301 FLOOR change; user-provided advisory (Phase 4.5 advisory: 15 flip root cause is_duplicate recompute record in spec_change_log.md recommended) is not yet executed. Without this distinction, audit-loop will mistake QA-301 effect=15 flips when true FLOOR effect is 12 flips.
findings.reproduction: Read backfill log, filter flipped array for soft_score_delta==0 -> 3 entries.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to docs/spec_change_log.md QA-301 entry: Phase 4.5 advisory (2026-05-30 post-fix verification): 15 flips breakdown = 12 FLOOR=0.90-driven + 3 is_duplicate retrofit (0506-026/0507-010/0507-013, all cluster overlaps with ATLAS-2026-0506-024/036). The latter are a side-effect of backfill incidentally repairing pre-QA-003 (9th audit) state and should be distinguished from the QA-301 FLOOR change effect. Implement together with QA-501 fix that adds cluster_cap_exceeded to failed_conditions.
findings.impact_scope: atlas-loop recommendation engine misestimates QA-301 effect size, distorting future Tier 2 FLOOR tuning decisions (e.g. 0.90 -> 0.95 rejection). Live impact 0.

findings.title: QA-302 (oos_is_pf_ratio full-period PF fallback) still deferred; combined with QA-301 enables soft_score double-penalize path
findings.description: result_parser.py:_compute_oos_is_ratio (240-263) falls back to full-period PF when is_pf is None, so strategies where IS PF cannot be computed in WFA walks get an artificial full_pf/full_pf=1.0 ratio reaching normalized=1.0 (positive bias). Meanwhile QA-301 FLOOR=0.90 penalizes strategies where WFA/Drift/Top3 are None through both this fallback and effective_weight floor. Same as AUDIT-2026-0530-001 QA-405 finding. Currently uncounted: how many of the 607 6.2.0 cohort use full-period fallback.
findings.reproduction: Need to count: layer2.is_profit_factor_walks=None AND oos_profit_factor != None (not executed in this session).
findings.suggested_fix: (1) Promote QA-302 from deferred to active finding; modify _compute_oos_is_ratio so when is_profit_factor_walks is None it returns normalized=0.0 (None-equivalent). (2) Before applying, count fallback-using strategies in 607 6.2.0 cohort and record double-penalize case count in spec_change_log. (3) Bump METRICS_SCHEMA_VERSION 6.2.0 -> 6.3.0 and backfill (incorporating QA-501/503/505 fixes too).
findings.impact_scope: Tier 2 PASS bias affects 607 strategies. For FTS-imported strategies, may distort future improvement loop recommendations. Live impact limited to imported scope.

findings.title: Backfill script lacks defensive guard for overall_passed=True yet tier2 missing anomaly
findings.description: backfill_v6_2_0_soft_score.py:135-145 unconditionally skips when gate_check.tier2 is missing. Current count: 0 strategies have overall_passed=True among 846 skipped (safe today), but future Phase-2-pre format strategies with overall_passed=True would also be silently skipped. Not assert-guarded, so future same-shape backfills risk overlooking such cases.
evidence: Session measurement: 0 strategies among 846 skipped with overall_passed=True
findings.reproduction: Scan all 1453 strategies: tier2 missing AND overall_passed=True -> 0.
findings.suggested_fix: Add guard in backfill_v6_2_0_soft_score.py:135-145: if status==skip_no_tier2 but overall_passed=True, raise AssertionError for manual investigation. Currently 0 occurrences but invariant-check protects future same-shape backfills. Add tests/unit/test_backfill_skip_invariants.py.
findings.impact_scope: Currently no real impact. Defensive line for future Tier 2 schema-change backfills.

findings.title: DESIGN-C-001 (RE-305 vs 12h throttle contradiction) has indirect impact only on ATLAS evaluation side
findings.description: DESIGN-C-001 is an FTS design decision and does not directly touch ATLAS backtest/scoring code paths. Strategies ATLAS generates may later be evaluated by FTS forward-test, where live_orders_silent alert throttling during outages might mask silent failures, but ATLAS scoring does not reflect this. Option A (dedicated kind separation) makes future cross-checks with ATLAS evaluation more robust. Logged as cross-cluster observation rather than a finding.
evidence: Grep live_orders_silent in C:/data/works/FX/ATLAS -> no hits
findings.reproduction: Grep live_orders_silent in C:/data/works/FX/ATLAS -> no matches.
findings.suggested_fix: DESIGN-C-001 option selection belongs to FTS. If ATLAS-loop later incorporates FTS forward-test results into backtest scoring, Option A (dedicated kind separation + continuous notification) avoids missing FTS signals during throttling.
findings.impact_scope: No real impact on ATLAS standalone. Live impact 0.
